score_details.score_summary: Muslim Bar Association of New Yorkinc shows moderate alignment and limited impact. Evidence score may reflect limited reporting capacity, not program weakness.

zakat.claim_evidence: No mention of "zakat" or "zakat donations" on the Muslim Bar Association of New York's website or related search results. The organization's mission and activities focus on organizing and representing Muslim attorneys, professional development, and advocacy for the Muslim community in New York and the US, rather than direct charitable distribution to zakat-eligible recipients. (Source: https://www.mubany.org/about-us)

all_citations.claim: The organization reports 9.0 months of working capital and total revenue of $93,638, though it lacks a formal Charity Navigator rating score.
The mission involves organizing Muslim attorneys in New York and conducting programs such as the Annual Gala and Annual Iftar.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization receives a moderate score of 41/100, reflecting a stable financial foundation but significant gaps in public data and impact reporting.
areas_for_improvement: Limited transparency regarding program expense ratios and specific impact metrics [1].
Low data confidence due to minimal third-party verification of outcomes [1].

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated at 23/50, representing a balanced but limited fit for general donors due to its specialized focus on professional advocacy rather than urgent humanitarian needs [1]. As a Sadaqah-eligible organization, it provides a niche platform for supporting the Muslim legal community, though its track record is primarily evidenced by social and networking events [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization's impact score of 18/50 is considered medium-low, as its work is categorized as indirect advocacy with no available data on cost per beneficiary [1]. While it successfully reported total revenue of $93,638, the lack of a reported program expense ratio makes it difficult to assess how efficiently funds are converted into services [1][2].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Bar Association of New York Inc. serves as a professional network dedicated to representing and advancing the interests of Muslim attorneys in the New York metropolitan area.
strengths: Maintains a solid liquidity position with 9.0 months of working capital [1].
Established presence in the New York legal community through recurring annual programs [2].
baseline_narrative.summary: The organization works to improve the position of Muslim legal professionals through advocacy and community-building initiatives [2]. Its primary activities include hosting an Annual Gala and an Annual Iftar to foster connections within the legal community [1][2].

all_citations.claim: resilient liquidity position with 9.0 months of working capital

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value professional institutional development and want to support the representation of Muslim attorneys within the American legal system.
donor_motivations: Want to strengthen the voice of Muslim professionals in the judiciary
Value networking platforms for minority legal practitioners
Support long-term advocacy over immediate humanitarian relief
giving_considerations: Donations support indirect advocacy and networking rather than direct services
The organization has a small financial footprint with limited impact data
Focus is localized to the New York legal community

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects <cite id="13">unverified outcomes</cite> typical of an emerging professional association, with a <cite id="2">basic documented theory of change</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: By organizing Muslim attorneys and providing networking and career resources, the organization aims to improve the professional standing and advocacy power of the Muslim community in the legal field.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence helps donors understand how professional networking translates into broader legal protections for the community.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="3">revenue under $100,000</cite> and a <cite id="20">significant funding gap</cite>, the organization has high capacity to absorb new donations for program expansion.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="8">9.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing a stable buffer for its professional programming.
MuBANY has demonstrated a <cite id="4">3-year revenue CAGR of 232.4%</cite>, indicating increasing community support and scale.
The association is a <cite id="7">founding member of the Muslim American Judicial Advisory Council (MAJAC)</cite>, enhancing its advocacy reach.

strengths_deep_dive: MuBANY's primary strength lies in its role as a specialized professional network. By facilitating high-profile events like the <cite id="5">Annual Gala and Annual Iftar</cite>, it creates a unique space for Muslim legal professionals to organize. Its financial health is characterized by <cite id="10">total assets of $73,097</cite> against a lean operating budget, allowing for <cite id="8">9.0 months of working capital</cite>. This fiscal conservatism ensures that the organization can sustain its <cite id="6">job board and membership services</cite> even during periods of fluctuating donation income.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim Bar Association of New York Inc. (MuBANY) is a professional membership organization dedicated to <cite id="1">organizing and representing Muslim attorneys</cite> in the New York metropolitan area. The organization focuses on advancing the goals and interests of Muslim legal professionals while <cite id="2">improving their position within the legal community</cite>. In the 2023 tax year, the organization reported <cite id="3">total revenue of $93,638</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="4">three-year revenue CAGR of 232.4%</cite>. MuBANY serves as a vital networking hub, hosting signature events such as an <cite id="5">Annual Gala and an Annual Iftar</cite> to foster professional connections. Beyond social networking, the association provides career support through a <cite id="6">job board and membership directory</cite>, and it has gained institutional recognition as a <cite id="7">founding member of the Muslim American Judicial Advisory Council</cite>. While the organization maintains a <cite id="8">resilient liquidity position with 9.0 months of working capital</cite>, it operates with a relatively small financial footprint and relies on <cite id="9">indirect delivery models</cite> to achieve its advocacy and community-building objectives.
